name: Septic Shock
diagnosis: Hypoxic respiratory failure with septic shock
cause: SpO2 below 90% with low BP, oxygen deprivation and sepsis
fix_steps: Switch to non-rebreather mask at 15 L/min
Draw blood cultures before starting antibiotics

name: Hypertension
fix_steps: Place patient in quiet room for rest
Notify physician if BP remains high

name: Hypoglycemia
fix_steps: Provide a small snack with complex carbs

name: Fever
cause: Core body temperature exceeding 38.5 degrees Celsius
fix_steps: Apply cool compresses to forehead and groin
Encourage oral hydration and fluid intake

name: Cardiac crisis
diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome or myocardial infarction
cause: Myocardial ischemia or coronary artery occlusion
fix_steps: Establish IV access and prepare defibrillator
Activate rapid response or cardiac team

name: Respiratory failure
cause: Inadequate gas exchange and severe oxygen desaturation
fix_steps: Initiate high-flow nasal cannula or bipap
Assess lung sounds and chest expansion

name: Stroke
cause: Cerebral vascular compromise and neurological deficit
fix_steps: Maintain patent airway and monitor neurological status

name: Arrhythmia
fix_steps: Assess patient's perfusion status and blood pressure
Prepare vagal maneuvers or pharmacological intervention

name: Diabetic crisis
diagnosis: Diabetic ketoacidosis or hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state
cause: Severe insulin deficiency and metabolic acidosis
fix_steps: Check blood glucose and electrolytes hourly
Monitor for signs of cerebral edema

name: Normal Vitals
diagnosis: Patient parameters are within normal physiological ranges.
fix_steps: Monitor vitals on next scheduled shift
